entries.name: Fitz Roy (Cerro Chaltén)
entries.desc: A jagged granite spire in Los Glaciares National Park on the Argentine-Chilean Patagonian border. Notoriously difficult to climb, it towers over the trekking hub of El Chaltén.

photo.alt: Fitz Roy granite spire at sunrise, seen from El Chaltén
photo.credit: <!-- photo: fitz-roy.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Fitz Roy El Chalten sunrise-13.jpg" by Almonroth · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Fitz_Roy_El_Chalten_sunrise-13.jpg -->

entries.name: Uluru (Ayers Rock)
entries.desc: A massive sandstone monolith rising from the desert of Australia's Red Centre, sacred to the Anangu people. A UNESCO World Heritage site, it glows red at sunrise and sunset.

photo.alt: Uluru sandstone monolith rising from the desert of Australia's Red Centre
photo.credit: <!-- photo: uluru.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Uluru CloseUp.JPG" by Stefano · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Uluru_CloseUp.JPG -->

entries.name: Kata Tjuta (The Olgas)
entries.desc: A group of large domed rock formations near Uluru in Australia's Northern Territory, sacred to the Anangu. Part of the same UNESCO park, they are explored via the Valley of the Winds walk.

photo.alt: Kata Tjuta domed rock formations near Uluru
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kata-tjuta.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Kata Tjuta - Valley of the Winds.jpg" by Tununda · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kata_Tjuta_-_Valley_of_the_Winds.jpg -->

entries.name: Bungle Bungles (Purnululu)
entries.desc: A range of orange-and-black-striped sandstone beehive domes in the remote Kimberley region of Western Australia. A UNESCO World Heritage site accessible by 4WD or helicopter, the domes were largely unknown outside the region until 1983.

photo.alt: Orange-and-black striped sandstone beehive domes of the Bungle Bungle Range
photo.credit: <!-- photo: bungle-bungles.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "The Bungle Bungles, Purnululu National Park.jpg" by Emily Cox · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Bungle_Bungles,_Purnululu_National_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Mount Robson
entries.desc: The highest peak in the Canadian Rockies, rising steeply in a British Columbia provincial park. The Berg Lake trail leads to glacier-fed lakes beneath its towering north face.

photo.alt: Mount Robson, highest peak in the Canadian Rockies, at twilight
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-robson.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Mount Robson Twilight.jpg" by Jeffrey Pang from Madison, NJ, USA · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Robson_Twilight.jpg -->

meta: Highest Peak in the Canadian Rockies

entries.name: Mount Assiniboine
entries.desc: A pyramidal peak on the BC-Alberta border, often called the Matterhorn of the Rockies for its shape. Its remote provincial park is reached only by a long hike or helicopter.

photo.alt: Mount Assiniboine, pyramidal peak on the BC-Alberta border
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-assiniboine.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Mount Assiniboine Provincial Park, Kootenays, Canada.jpg" by BenHWilson · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Assiniboine_Provincial_Park,_Kootenays,_Canada.jpg -->

entries.name: Torres del Paine
entries.desc: A range of granite towers and horns in Chilean Patagonia, centerpiece of Torres del Paine National Park. Its multi-day W and O treks are among the world's most celebrated hikes.

photo.alt: Granite towers of Torres del Paine in Chilean Patagonia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: torres-del-paine.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Torres del Paine-Chile.jpg" by Emilia Hölck · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Torres_del_Paine-Chile.jpg -->

entries.name: Osorno Volcano
entries.desc: A near-perfect snow-capped volcanic cone in Chile's Lake District, often compared to Mount Fuji. It rises above Lake Llanquihue and offers skiing and views across the surrounding lakes.

photo.alt: Snow-capped cone of Osorno Volcano above Lake Llanquihue
photo.credit: <!-- photo: osorno-volcano.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.5 · "Volcano Osorno and Petrohué waterfalls.JPG" by Ruben Lohaus Brito · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Volcano_Osorno_and_Petrohu%C3%A9_waterfalls.JPG -->

entries.name: Zhangjiajie Pillars
entries.desc: Thousands of towering quartz-sandstone pillars in Zhangjiajie National Forest Park in Hunan, China. Often shrouded in mist, they helped inspire the floating mountains in Avatar.

photo.alt: Towering sandstone karst pillars rising from Wulingyuan, Zhangjiajie
photo.credit: <!-- photo: zhangjiajie-pillars.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "1 tianzishan wulingyuan zhangjiajie 2012.jpg" by chensiyuan · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1_tianzishan_wulingyuan_zhangjiajie_2012.jpg -->

entries.name: Huangshan (Yellow Mountains)
entries.desc: A UNESCO-listed granite range in Anhui, China, famed for jagged peaks, twisted pines, and seas of cloud. Long a subject of Chinese painting and poetry, it is reached by cable car and stone stairways.

photo.alt: Jagged granite peaks of Huangshan, Anhui
photo.credit: <!-- photo: huangshan.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 IGO · "Mount Huangshan-110984.jpg" by Giovanni Boccardi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Huangshan-110984.jpg -->

entries.name: Mount Sinai (Jebel Musa)
entries.desc: A peak in Egypt's South Sinai, traditionally identified with the biblical mountain where Moses received the commandments. Many hike it overnight to watch sunrise from the summit.

photo.alt: Sunrise view from the summit of Mount Sinai, South Sinai
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-sinai.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Sunrise from the summit of Mount Sinai or Gabal Musa.jpg" by Sara Nabih · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sunrise_from_the_summit_of_Mount_Sinai_or_Gabal_Musa.jpg -->

entries.name: Mont Blanc
entries.desc: The highest mountain in the Alps and in Western Europe, on the France-Italy border. A birthplace of mountaineering, it is circled by the classic Tour du Mont Blanc trek.

photo.alt: Mont Blanc massif as seen from Chamonix
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mont-blanc.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Mont Blanc depuis Chamonix.JPG" by Aiguille · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mont_Blanc_depuis_Chamonix.JPG -->

entries.name: Piton de la Fournaise
entries.desc: A shield volcano on Réunion Island in the Indian Ocean, one of the most active volcanoes on Earth. Frequent eruptions within its caldera draw hikers to viewpoints along the rim.

photo.alt: Dolomieu Crater at the summit of Piton de la Fournaise, Réunion
photo.credit: <!-- photo: piton-de-la-fournaise.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Réunion Fournaise Dolomieu2009.JPG" by B.navez · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:R%C3%A9union_Fournaise_Dolomieu2009.JPG -->

entries.name: Meteora
entries.desc: A complex of enormous sandstone pillars rising from the Thessaly plain in central Greece, their summits bearing six active Eastern Orthodox monasteries. A UNESCO dual World Heritage site combining natural and cultural significance.

photo.alt: Great Meteoron Monastery perched atop a sandstone pillar, Thessaly
photo.credit: <!-- photo: meteora.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Great Meteora Monastery (4694771100).jpg" by Francisco Anzola · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Great_Meteora_Monastery_(4694771100).jpg -->

entries.name: Kirkjufell
entries.desc: A steep, cone-shaped peak on the Snæfellsnes peninsula in western Iceland, rising beside a coastal waterfall. Often called the most photographed mountain in the country.

photo.alt: Kirkjufell mountain on the Snæfellsnes peninsula, Iceland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kirkjufell.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Kirkjufell Mountain, Grundarfjörður, Iceland (42317168545).jpg" by Joe deSousa · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kirkjufell_Mountain,_Grundarfj%C3%B6r%C3%B0ur,_Iceland_(42317168545).jpg -->

entries.name: Tre Cime di Lavaredo
entries.desc: Three distinctive rock towers in the Sexten Dolomites of northern Italy, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Their sheer walls are iconic in the Alps and ringed by a popular loop trail.

photo.alt: The three rock towers of Tre Cime di Lavaredo in the Sexten Dolomites
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tre-cime-di-lavaredo.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Drei Zinnen Tre Cime di Lavaredo Dolomites.jpg" by Wolfgang Moroder · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Drei_Zinnen_Tre_Cime_di_Lavaredo_Dolomites.jpg -->

entries.name: Seceda Ridgeline
entries.desc: A sawtooth ridgeline above Val Gardena in the South Tyrol Dolomites, reached by cable car from Ortisei. Its jagged silhouette over green alpine meadows is a signature Dolomite view.

photo.alt: Sawtooth ridgeline of the Odle peaks above Seceda in the Dolomites
photo.credit: <!-- photo: seceda-ridgeline-dolomites.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Seceda e nuvole" by Luigi Rossini · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Seceda_e_nuvole.jpg -->

entries.name: Mount Etna
entries.desc: The tallest active volcano in Europe, dominating eastern Sicily above Catania. Among the world's most active volcanoes, it is a UNESCO site with frequent eruptions and lava flows.

photo.alt: Snow-capped summit of Mount Etna rising above the Sicilian countryside
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-etna.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Mount Etna from the south 060313" by Wilson44691 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Etna_from_the_south_060313.JPG -->

entries.name: Mount Fuji
entries.desc: Japan's highest peak, an active volcano with a near-symmetrical cone that is a national symbol and UNESCO World Heritage site. It draws climbers during a short summer season.

photo.alt: Mount Fuji's snow-capped cone reflected in Lake Kawaguchi
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-fuji.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Mount Fuji from Lake Kawaguchi s2" by Alpsdake · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Fuji_from_Lake_Kawaguchi_s2.jpg -->

entries.name: Wadi Rum
entries.desc: A desert valley of sandstone mountains and cliffs in southern Jordan, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Its dramatic landscape draws climbers, hikers, and visitors to Bedouin desert camps.

photo.alt: Sandstone mountains and cliffs rising from the Wadi Rum desert floor
photo.credit: <!-- photo: wadi-rum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Wadi Rum, Jordan, Mountains" by Vyacheslav Argenberg · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Wadi_Rum,_Jordan,_Mountains.jpg -->

entries.name: Tsingy de Bemaraha
entries.desc: A vast limestone plateau in western Madagascar eroded into a dense forest of razor-sharp karst needles, some reaching 120 meters. A UNESCO World Heritage site accessible only by guided traverse via bridges and ladders.

photo.alt: Dense forest of razor-sharp limestone karst needles at Tsingy de Bemaraha
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tsingy-de-bemaraha.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Relief karstique, Tsingy de Bemaraha, Madagascar" by Cactus0625 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Relief_karstique,_Tsingy_de_Bemaraha,_Madagascar.jpg -->

entries.name: Mount Kinabalu
entries.desc: The highest peak in Southeast Asia's Malay Archipelago, in a UNESCO-listed national park in Sabah, Borneo. Its granite summit is reached by a popular two-day guided climb.

photo.alt: Granite summit spires of Mount Kinabalu
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-kinabalu.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Mount Kinabalu 1" by ReubenST · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Kinabalu_1.jpg -->

entries.name: Spitzkoppe
entries.desc: A group of bold granite inselbergs rising from the Namib Desert plains in northwest Namibia, the tallest reaching 1,728 meters. Known as the Matterhorn of Namibia, the boulders shelter ancient San rock paintings and make a dramatic desert campsite.

photo.alt: Granite inselberg of Spitzkoppe rising from the Namib Desert plains
photo.credit: <!-- photo: spitzkoppe.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Grosse Spitzkoppe - Namibie - panoramio" by Pavel Špindler · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Grosse_Spitzkoppe_-_Namibie_-_panoramio.jpg -->

entries.name: Mount Everest (Sagarmatha / Chomolungma)
entries.desc: The highest mountain on Earth above sea level, on the Nepal-Tibet border in the Himalayas. First summited in 1953, it anchors the famous Everest Base Camp trek.

photo.alt: Summit pyramid of Mount Everest above the Himalayas
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-everest.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Everest, Himalayas" by Vyacheslav Argenberg · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Everest,_Himalayas.jpg -->

entries.name: Machhapuchhre (Fishtail Peak)
entries.desc: A striking twin-summited peak in the Annapurna Himalayas near Pokhara, named for its fishtail shape. Considered sacred, it has never been officially climbed to the summit.

photo.alt: Machhapuchhre (Fishtail Peak) rising above Pokhara, Nepal
photo.credit: <!-- photo: machhapuchhre.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Machhapuchhre Himal Pokhara.jpg" by Amogh Risal · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Machhapuchhre_Himal_Pokhara.jpg -->

entries.name: Ama Dablam
entries.desc: A dramatic peak in the Khumbu region of the Nepal Himalayas, prized for its sharp ridges and steep faces. Its distinctive profile is a highlight of the Everest Base Camp trek.

photo.alt: Ama Dablam peak in the Khumbu Himalaya, Nepal
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ama-dablam.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Ama Dablam, Nepal.jpg" by Vyacheslav Argenberg · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ama_Dablam,_Nepal.jpg -->

entries.name: Milford Sound (Mitre Peak)
entries.desc: A fjord in Fiordland National Park on New Zealand's South Island, framed by the sheer Mitre Peak. Carved by glaciers and fed by waterfalls, it is a highlight of the Milford Track.

photo.alt: Mitre Peak rising above Milford Sound, Fiordland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: milford-sound-mitre-peak.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Mitre Peak with snow at the top, reflecting in the water.jpg" by Pseudopanax at English Wikipedia · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mitre_Peak_with_snow_at_the_top,_reflecting_in_the_water.jpg -->

entries.name: Aoraki / Mount Cook
entries.desc: The highest mountain in New Zealand, a glaciated peak in the Southern Alps of the South Island. Its national park offers accessible valley walks such as the Hooker Valley track.

photo.alt: Aoraki / Mount Cook, New Zealand's tallest peak
photo.credit: <!-- photo: aoraki-mount-cook.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Aoraki Mount Cook.JPG" by C.M. Lynch · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Aoraki_Mount_Cook.JPG -->

entries.name: Trolltunga
entries.desc: A flat rock slab jutting horizontally from a cliff high above Ringedalsvatnet lake in western Norway. Reached only by a long, strenuous day hike, it is a famous fjord-country viewpoint.

photo.alt: Trolltunga rock ledge above Ringedalsvatnet, Norway
photo.credit: <!-- photo: trolltunga.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Trolltunga.jpg" by Jan Skrodzki · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Trolltunga.jpg -->

entries.name: Preikestolen (Pulpit Rock)
entries.desc: A flat-topped cliff that drops sheer to the Lysefjord in Ryfylke, Norway. One of the country's most visited natural landmarks, reached by a well-trodden half-day hike.

photo.alt: Preikestolen cliff above the Lysefjord, Norway
photo.credit: <!-- photo: preikestolen.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Preikestolen 1.jpg" by Stefan Krause, Germany · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Preikestolen_1.jpg -->

entries.name: K2 (Mount Godwin-Austen)
entries.desc: The second-highest mountain in the world, in the Karakoram Range on the China-Pakistan border. Steeper and far deadlier to climb than Everest, it is nicknamed the Savage Mountain.

photo.alt: K2, the second-highest mountain in the world, Karakoram Range
photo.credit: <!-- photo: k2.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "K2, Mount Godwin Austen, Chogori, Savage Mountain.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:K2,_Mount_Godwin_Austen,_Chogori,_Savage_Mountain.jpg -->

entries.name: Vinicunca (Rainbow Mountain)
entries.desc: A high Andean ridge near Cusco banded in vivid colors from layered mineral sediments. Sitting above 5,000 meters, it is reached by a demanding high-altitude day hike from Cusco.

photo.alt: Vinicunca, the banded Rainbow Mountain near Cusco
photo.credit: <!-- photo: vinicunca-rainbow-mountain.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Vinicunca, Rainbow Mountain.jpg" by Yifan Wang · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Vinicunca,_Rainbow_Mountain.jpg -->

entries.name: Virunga Volcanoes
entries.desc: A chain of eight volcanoes along the borders of Rwanda, Uganda, and DR Congo in the Albertine Rift. Their forested slopes are a last refuge of the endangered mountain gorilla.

photo.alt: Mount Mikeno and Mount Karisimbi, two volcanoes in the Virunga mountain range
photo.credit: <!-- photo: virunga-volcanoes.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Mount Mikeno (DR Congo) and Mount Karisimbi (Rwanda), two extinct volcanoes in the Virunga mountain range." by MONUSCO Photos · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Mikeno_(DR_Congo)_and_Mount_Karisimbi_(Rwanda),_two_extinct_volcanoes_in_the_Virunga_mountain_range._(19556231290).jpg -->

entries.name: Table Mountain
entries.desc: A flat-topped sandstone massif overlooking Cape Town, forming the city's defining backdrop. A rotating cable car and numerous trails lead to its broad summit plateau.

photo.alt: Table Mountain overlooking Cape Town from the waterfront
photo.credit: <!-- photo: table-mountain.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Cape Town - Table Mountain seen from Waterfront (1)" by Romaine · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cape_Town_-_Table_Mountain_seen_from_Waterfront_(1).jpg -->

entries.name: Matterhorn (Cervino)
entries.desc: An almost symmetrical pyramidal peak on the Swiss-Italian border in the Pennine Alps. One of the most recognizable mountains in the world, it rises above the resort town of Zermatt.

photo.alt: The Matterhorn peak rising above Zermatt village
photo.credit: <!-- photo: matterhorn.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "View of the Matterhorn from Zermatt village" by TiagoSanchez · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:View_of_the_Matterhorn_from_Zermatt_village.jpg -->

entries.name: Eiger North Face
entries.desc: A peak in the Bernese Alps whose immense north face is one of the most notorious climbs in mountaineering history. The Jungfrau Railway tunnels through the mountain to Jungfraujoch.

photo.alt: The north face of the Eiger above Grindelwald
photo.credit: <!-- photo: eiger-north-face.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Eiger North face from Grindelwald 2016-09-02 n1" by Marie-Lan Nguyen · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Eiger_North_face_from_Grindelwald_2016-09-02_n1.jpg -->

entries.name: Mount Kilimanjaro
entries.desc: The highest mountain in Africa and the tallest free-standing mountain in the world, a dormant volcano in northern Tanzania. Its summit is reachable by trekkers without technical climbing.

photo.alt: Mount Kilimanjaro seen from Moshi, Tanzania
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-kilimanjaro.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Mount Kilimanjaro,Moshi" by Erasmus Kamugisha · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Kilimanjaro,Moshi.jpg -->

entries.name: Cappadocia Fairy Chimneys
entries.desc: Thousands of volcanic tuff pillars — capped by harder rock — eroded across the valleys of Cappadocia in central Turkey, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Many were hollowed out as homes, churches, and underground cities over centuries.

photo.alt: Volcanic tuff fairy chimneys in Göreme Valley, Cappadocia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cappadocia-fairy-chimneys.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Göreme Valley in Cappadocia edit1" by Brocken Inaglory · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:G%C3%B6reme_Valley_in_Cappadocia_edit1.jpg -->

entries.name: Grand Teton
entries.desc: The highest peak of the Teton Range in Wyoming, rising abruptly from the valley floor without foothills. Its sharp granite summit is a landmark of Grand Teton National Park.

photo.alt: Grand Teton rising abruptly above the valley floor in Wyoming
photo.credit: <!-- photo: grand-teton.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Grand Teton and Teewinot 1" by Acroterion · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Grand_Teton_and_Teewinot_1.jpg -->

meta: Highest Peak in the Teton Range

entries.name: Monument Valley
entries.desc: A cluster of towering red sandstone buttes on the Arizona-Utah border within the Navajo Nation. Its formations are icons of the American Southwest, seen in countless films.

photo.alt: The Mittens buttes in Monument Valley on the Arizona-Utah border
photo.credit: <!-- photo: monument-valley.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "The Mittens, Monument Valley, Utah-Arizona, Down from the Visitor Center at the Navajo Tribal Park" by Alex Proimos · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Mittens,_Monument_Valley,_Utah-Arizona,_Down_from_the_Visitor_Center_at_the_Navajo_Tribal_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Devils Tower
entries.desc: A flat-topped igneous rock tower rising abruptly above the plains of northeastern Wyoming. The first United States national monument, its fluted columns are a magnet for rock climbers.

photo.alt: Devils Tower rising above the Wyoming plains
photo.credit: <!-- photo: devils-tower.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Devils Tower CROP.jpg" by Colin Faulkingham · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Devils_Tower_CROP.jpg -->

entries.name: Half Dome
entries.desc: A granite dome in Yosemite National Park with a sheer near-vertical face rising above the valley. Its cable-assisted summit route is one of the park's most demanding day hikes.

photo.alt: Half Dome's granite cliff face above Yosemite Valley
photo.credit: <!-- photo: half-dome.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Half dome yosemite nationalpark.JPG" by HylgeriaK · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Half_dome_yosemite_nationalpark.JPG -->

entries.name: Denali (Mount McKinley)
entries.desc: The highest peak in North America, at the heart of Denali National Park in Alaska. Measured from base to summit, its vertical rise is among the greatest of any mountain on land.

photo.alt: Denali (Mount McKinley) rising over Denali National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: denali.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Mt. McKinley, Denali National Park.jpg" by Nic McPhee · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mt._McKinley,_Denali_National_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Mount Rainier
entries.desc: A glacier-clad active volcano and the highest peak in Washington's Cascade Range. It looms over the Seattle region and is circled by the 150-kilometer Wonderland Trail.

photo.alt: Mount Rainier's glacier-clad summit over the Cascades
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-rainier.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Mount Rainier over Tacoma.jpg" by Lyn Topinka (USGS) · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Rainier_over_Tacoma.jpg -->

entries.name: Antelope Canyon
entries.desc: A flowing sandstone slot canyon on Navajo Nation land near Page, Arizona, carved by flash floods into wave-like passages of red and orange rock. Upper and Lower Antelope are accessible only on guided Navajo tours.

photo.alt: Antelope Canyon's wave-like sandstone slot canyon walls
photo.credit: <!-- photo: antelope-canyon.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "USA Antelope-Canyon.jpg" by Lucas Löffler · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:USA_Antelope-Canyon.jpg -->

entries.name: Bryce Canyon Hoodoos
entries.desc: The world's largest concentration of hoodoos — tall limestone spires eroded into orange, red, and white columns — fills the amphitheaters of Bryce Canyon National Park in southern Utah.

photo.alt: Bryce Canyon amphitheater filled with orange hoodoo spires
photo.credit: <!-- photo: bryce-canyon-hoodoos.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Bryce Canyon Amphitheater Hoodoos Panorama.jpg" by Jonathan Zander (Digon3) · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bryce_Canyon_Amphitheater_Hoodoos_Panorama.jpg -->

entries.name: Mount Roraima
entries.desc: The highest of the tepui table mountains, where Venezuela, Brazil, and Guyana meet in the Gran Sabana. Its sheer-walled plateau, home to unique species, inspired The Lost World.

photo.alt: Mount Roraima's sheer-walled tepui plateau
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-roraima.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Monte Roraima, Roraima.JPG" by Caio Vilela · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Monte_Roraima,_Roraima.JPG -->

entries.name: Auyán-tepui (Angel Falls)
entries.desc: A vast tepui in Venezuela's Canaima National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage site. From its edge plunges Angel Falls, the tallest uninterrupted waterfall on Earth.

photo.alt: Angel Falls plunging from Auyán-tepui in Canaima National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: angel-falls.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Angel Falls in Venezuela.jpg" by Luis Carillo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Angel_Falls_in_Venezuela.jpg -->

entries.name: Ha Long Bay Karst
entries.desc: A seascape of roughly 1,600 limestone karst towers rising from the Gulf of Tonkin in northern Vietnam, a UNESCO World Heritage site. The formations — riddled with caves and grottoes — are explored by overnight junk cruises.

photo.alt: Limestone karst towers rising from the emerald waters of Ha Long Bay, Vietnam
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ha-long-bay-karst.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.5 · "Ha Long Bay.jpg" by Ondřej Žváček · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ha_Long_Bay.jpg -->
